results.possibleIdentity: Erwin William Tschudi (1898–1978) was a physicist and mathematician associated with Hofstra University and the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization. He earned a Master of Arts from the University of Cincinnati in 1921 and a PhD in Physics from Johns Hopkins University in 1925. His early research focused on the physics of impact, with notable publications in 'Physical Review' including 'Duration of Impact of Two Spheres' and 'Duration of Impact of Bars' (1921). During the late 1960s and early 1970s, he was active in the fields of mathematics, physics, optics, and light, and he resided in Baldwin, New York. His technical background in optics and light likely facilitated his participation in E.A.T., a service organization promoting collaborations between artists and engineers. He served as a faculty member at Hofstra University in Hempstead, New York, which is located near his residence in Baldwin.
